text.title: AI Crawler Configuration
text.summary: llms.txt format and robots.txt configuration for AI crawlers (GPTBot, Claude-Web, PerplexityBot) — including citation format preferences, important page declarations, and URL structure requirements for AI parsing.

section.title: llms.txt File

rule.statement: Create /llms.txt at the domain root to guide AI crawlers: list important pages, specify citation format, declare allowed and disallowed paths, and provide AI-specific guidance not covered by robots.txt.
rule.rationale: robots.txt governs crawl access; llms.txt governs AI understanding and citation behavior. Without llms.txt, AI crawlers make assumptions about important pages and citation format that may not match your preferences.

example.note: llms.txt with crawler guidance and citation format.
example.code:
# llms.txt - AI crawler guidance User-agent: * Allow: /content/ Disallow: /admin/ Disallow: /premium/
# Preferred citation format Citation-style: [Title](URL) - Author
# Key pages for entity understanding Important: /about Important: /methodology Important: /research

section.reference: Full llms.txt template with all standard sections

rule.statement: Use clean, descriptive URLs: hyphens not underscores, no session IDs, no hash routing, under 100 characters, consistent patterns across content types.
rule.rationale: AI crawlers parse URL structure to infer content hierarchy and topic. Hash-based or session-parameterized URLs are not reliably indexed. Inconsistent patterns reduce AI confidence in entity-to-URL mapping.

list.item: GPTBot: OpenAI's crawler for ChatGPT training and browsing.
Googlebot: indexes for Google SGE (AI Overviews).

rule.statement: Configure separate robots.txt rules for AI crawlers to allow public content and block premium, admin, and user-generated content paths.
rule.rationale: AI crawlers should access your authoritative content but not private or paywalled material. Blanket blocking all AI crawlers prevents visibility in AI search results; blanket allowing them risks exposure of private content.
